name: json-assistant
description: Jump to any nested JSON path instantly, browse a live JSON outline in the sidebar, and find every code reference to a path across your workspace.

commands.title: JSON Tools: Find References to Path
JSON Tools: Create Merge Instruction for AI

viewsWelcome.contents:
Open a JSON or JSONC file to see its structure here, like the built-in Outline view but JSON-aware.

Click any entry to jump to it in the editor.

Use the $(search) filter icon above to narrow the tree down to matching keys, indices, or values — matches and their ancestors stay visible, everything else is hidden.

Right-click an entry and choose **Find References** to search your workspace for code that accesses that path.

[Search Path in Active File](command:jsonTools.searchPath)

Open a JSON or JSONC file to check for duplicate keys.

When duplicate keys are found, they are listed here with all their occurrences and values. Click an occurrence to jump to it in the editor.

Right-click a duplicate and choose **Ignore this duplicate** to hide it from this list. Use the $(list-unordered) icon above to view and remove entries from the ignore list.

jsonTools.languageIds.description: Language IDs treated as JSON for this extension's features.

jsonTools.referenceFinder.include.description: Glob of files to scan when finding references to a JSON path.

jsonTools.referenceFinder.exclude.description: Additional glob to exclude from reference scanning, on top of files.exclude/search.exclude.

jsonTools.referenceFinder.maxResults.description: Maximum number of reference matches to return.

jsonTools.referenceFinder.accessorFuncti.description: Function names treated as path-accessor calls, e.g. get('a.b.c').

jsonTools.outline.debounceMs.description: Debounce delay in milliseconds for the outline search filter.

jsonTools.duplicateKeys.mergeInstruction.default:
File: {filePath}

Merge duplicate key "{keyName}" found at multiple locations:
{locations}

Consider refactoring to eliminate duplication by:
- Creating a shared reference
- Extracting to a common parent object
- Using a data structure that deduplicates this key
jsonTools.duplicateKeys.mergeInstruction.description: Template for merge instructions for duplicate keys at the same path. Placeholders: {filePath}, {keyName}, {locations}

jsonTools.duplicateKeys.mergeInstruction.default:
File: {filePath}

Merge duplicated attribute "{attribute}" found in {pathCount} different locations:
{locations}

Consider refactoring to:
- Extract the attribute to a shared parent
- Create a template or base object
- Use composition to avoid repetition
jsonTools.duplicateKeys.mergeInstruction.description: Template for merge instructions for duplicate attributes. Placeholders: {filePath}, {attribute}, {pathCount}, {locations}

jsonTools.duplicateKeys.mergeInstruction.default:
File: {filePath}

Merge duplicated pair "{key}: {value}" found at {occurrenceCount} locations:
{locations}

Consider refactoring to:
- Extract to a shared constant or reference
- Use a single definition referenced multiple times
- Consolidate into a lookup table
jsonTools.duplicateKeys.mergeInstruction.description: Template for merge instructions for duplicate key-value pairs (leaf values only). Placeholders: {filePath}, {key}, {value}, {occurrenceCount}, {locations}

jsonTools.duplicateKeys.mergeInstruction.default:
File: {filePath}

Merge duplicated value "{value}" found at {occurrenceCount} locations:
{locations}

Consider refactoring to:
- Replace with a reference to a shared value
- Create a constants object
- Use variable substitution
jsonTools.duplicateKeys.mergeInstruction.description: Template for merge instructions for duplicate values. Placeholders: {filePath}, {value}, {occurrenceCount}, {locations}

scripts.test: npm run test:unit && npm run test:integration
scripts.pretest:integration: npm run compile && npm run compile-tests
